report_markdown:
## Week of May 18, 2026

You received 95 emails this week and sent zero. Your calendar had nothing scheduled. Your Todoist account has 14 tasks in it — every single one a tutorial stub from when you signed up 6 weeks ago — and you completed none of them. On paper this looks like a quiet week. It wasn't.

**You are frozen, not resting.**

The State Farm agent emailed you three times about renter's insurance — May 15, a follow-up that same day, then again May 21 — for a policy that costs $9.17 a month. You need it before June 1. You haven't replied. This is the most concrete thing in your data: one low-friction task with a hard deadline, three prompts to act, zero movement. In April you were sending emails at midnight, 11 unanswered threads deep. Now you've gone fully silent. That's not recovery. That's the other direction.

**The load this week was invisible but real.**

Three companies rejected your applications — Turquoise (May 21), a backend SWE role (May 21 early), and an AI engineer position — while you were simultaneously paying $1,760 in rent on May 23 for an apartment you haven't moved into, enrolling in a rent rewards program, setting up Seattle City Light utilities, and watching apartment listing emails pile up even after you've already signed a lease. You have a flight booked for May 30. You have 6 days. The cognitive load isn't from any single thing — it's from 6 major logistics threads running at once with no system to track them.

**The pattern across both weeks you've given Clarity is consistent.**

April: personal and financial tasks avoided, moved repeatedly, no system trusted. May: system abandoned entirely. The avoidance didn't start this week — it just ran out of things to avoid *around*.

---

The root cause is a backlog of avoided action masquerading as a rest period during a high-stakes transition month.

**The one honest thing:** Reply to the State Farm email today. Not because it's the most important thing in your life — but because it's the one thing where delay has a specific, irreversible consequence in 7 days, and it takes 4 minutes.
root_cause: Avoidance-as-default response to simultaneous high-stakes transitions (job search + cross-country move) is producing zero task completion and zero outbound communication during the week it matters most.
